Illuminating Data’s Story

Where statistical rigor meets pedagogical artistry—transforming complex data into profound understanding.

<p class="hero-badge-text">Warren Miller Prize Laureate</p>

Biography

I am an Associate Professor of Quantitative Methods in the Atkinson Graduate School of Management at Willamette University. My research weaves together panel and cross-sectional time series data, causal inference in observed populations (in collaboration with Tim Johnson), political economy, and the art of statistical computing.

As an Honorary Instructor at the University of Essex, I annually share insights at the Essex Summer School in Social Science Data Analysis. My academic journey encompasses distinguished positions at Dartmouth College, Harvard University, Texas A&M University, Washington University in Saint Louis, and Rice University.

Honored with the Warren Miller Prize for Statistical Backwards Induction— recognizing the finest contribution to Political Analysis.

Research Interests

Causal Inference

Panel Data Analysis

Political Economy

Statistical Computing

Applied Statistics

Bayesian Methods
